task: Search GitHub for the most starred Python CLI frameworks using the API: curl -s 'https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q=cli+framework+language:python&sort=stars&per_page=5'. Write repo names and star counts to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Search for npm packages related to 'terminal' using the registry API: curl -s 'https://registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search?text=terminal&size=5'. Extract package names and descriptions. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Look up a Python package on PyPI: curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/textual/json. Extract: name, version, author, summary, and license. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Get geolocation info for the public IP using: curl -s https://ipinfo.io/json. Extract: IP, city, region, country, org. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Look up domain information for ikangai.com: DNS records (dig), WHOIS registrar, and HTTP headers. Compile a domain report to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Look up recent CVEs (security vulnerabilities) using the NIST NVD API: curl -s 'https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?keywordSearch=python&resultsPerPage=3'. Extract CVE IDs and descriptions. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Check if Homebrew is installed. If yes, search for 'tmux' (brew info tmux) and write the installed version, dependencies, and description to /tmp/clive/result.txt. If not, write 'HOMEBREW_NOT_AVAILABLE'.
Generate a cheat sheet for 5 commonly used commands: curl, jq, awk, sed, grep. For each, show the 3 most useful options with examples. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Fetch the current time in multiple timezones using http://worldtimeapi.org/api/timezone/Europe/Berlin and http://worldtimeapi.org/api/timezone/America/New_York. Show the time difference and both current times. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Identify the license of the current git repository by reading LICENSE or LICENSE.md file. Also check if there's a license field in pyproject.toml or package.json. Write the license type to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
